steps.title: Solve for n to understand the equation
steps.content:
Starting with $2n + 6 = 14$, subtract 6: $2n = 8$, then divide by 2: $n = 4$.
The tree took 4 years to grow from 6 feet to 14 feet.

steps.title: Interpret the structure of the equation
steps.content:
The equation models the tree height over time. The 6 is the starting height. The term $2n$ adds height over $n$ years. Since height = starting height + (growth per year) × (years), the coefficient 2 must be growth per year.
Check: $14 - 6 = 8$ feet of growth over 4 years $= 2$ feet/year.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: Years to double its height — the tree doubled from 6 to 12 feet; that is not what 2 represents in the equation. Incorrect.
  • B: Average feet grown per year — 2 is the coefficient of $n$ (years), so it is the rate of growth in feet per year. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: Height when the tree was 1 year old — substituting $n = 1$ gives $2(1) + 6 = 8$ feet, not 2. Incorrect.
  • D: Average years for similar trees to grow 14 feet — the 2 is not a time value; it is a growth rate. Incorrect.
